ground.what_this_is: This is the 'Wire Failure & Lifecycle States' dashboard, a view that surfaces six specific types of build failures or non-closing outcomes that have returned to the operator for review.
ground.what_you_can_do: You can read the detailed reason for each failure (such as scope overrun, launch crash, or design unverifiability) and take action to resolve or escalate each item.

howto.how_to_change: The verdict surfacing is the decision→implementation wire (guarded close + surface_review); changing the verdict state requires a code change in the review verdicts logic.
howto.what: A status indicator on the build-review card showing a build that failed or was refused.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the reason for the failure (e.g., scope overrun, launch crash) and take action to resolve or escalate the item.

dossier.represents: A failed or refused build verdict needing operator action

howto.how_to_change: The state is determined by the review verdict logic in suite.py:review_verdicts; changing the visible label requires updating that verdict logic.
howto.what: A verdict marker on the build-review card indicating the item's current state.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the status ('held', 'deferred', 're-offered') to understand the item's lifecycle stage and required action.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific label yet.
howto.what: A status indicator on the build-review card showing the design is partially complete.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the visual cue to understand the design's current state (half-finished) within the review context.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to it yet.
howto.what: A hidden control on the build-review card that opens the backstage system.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click it to open the backstage interface (mockup-only; not a built feature here).

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific text yet.
howto.what: A status indicator showing the current implementation state of a reviewed wire.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the verdict status ('no implemented') to understand the current lifecycle state of the review item.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific label yet.
howto.what: The default verdict label on a build-review card.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the system's default stance ('deny-all') for the current build review; this is a static label indicating the initial state before operator action.

howto.how_to_change: The stamp is a surface element on the review card; changing its text or meaning requires updating the design verdict logic in the review wire (runtime/suite.py:review_verdicts).
howto.what: A visual stamp on the build review card indicating the design team's assessment.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the design team's verdict (e.g., 'design review') to understand the status of the design phase before proceeding to implementation.

howto.how_to_change: The rationale text is the visible organ; the logic is guarded in the build-intent wire.
howto.what: A design rationale card explaining why a build change cannot auto-close.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the reason that the change requires design review before logic is considered done.

howto.how_to_change: The status is surfaced by the review verdicts wire; changing the underlying verification logic requires a code change in the review verdicts region.
howto.what: A status indicator on the build-review card showing that the form is currently unverifiable.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the current state of the form's verification status to understand why the build cannot be finalized yet.

howto.how_to_change: This is a mockup-only surface; no built code path maps to this specific failure message yet.
howto.what: A status message indicating a build attempt failed to start cleanly and was automatically re-queued for review.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the failure reason to understand why the build was rejected and that it is now waiting in the queue.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the build-review region (runtime/suite.py:review_verdicts); changing the hold action is a code change there.
howto.what: A hold button on the build-review card.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click it to pause the current review state without resolving the build, keeping the item in a suspended lifecycle.

howto.how_to_change: The stamp is a visual state on the review card; changing the deferral logic requires modifying the review verdicts wire (runtime/suite.py:review_verdicts).
howto.what: A status stamp indicating a build review has been paused or deferred.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the reason for the deferral and wait for further instructions; it does not trigger a new build.

dossier.represents: A deferred verdict stamp on a build review

howto.how_to_change: The text is a mockup-only surface; no built code path maps to this specific alert message yet.
howto.what: A status indicator on the build review card showing that the number of ready builds exceeds the system's concurrency limit.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the warning that builds are waiting due to capacity constraints; this informs the operator that they cannot approve more builds until the queue drains.

howto.how_to_change: This control lives in the build-review region (runtime/suite.py:review_verdicts); changing its behavior requires a code update to that region.
howto.what: A control on the build-review card that initiates a 'raise the cap' action.
howto.what_you_can_do: Clicking this action signals the system to escalate or re-evaluate the current build failure state, effectively 'raising the cap' on the review scope.

howto.how_to_change: The claim surfacing is the decision→implementation wire (guarded close + surface_review); the specific claim logic lives in the build-review region (runtime/suite.py:review_verdicts).
howto.what: A specific claim card for a build that was claimed but lost mid-flight.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the narrative of the vanished build and take action to resolve or escalate this specific claim.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the build-review region (runtime/suite.py:review_verdicts); adding this action requires a code change there to expose the re-trigger wire.
howto.what: A button on the build-review card that allows the operator to re-trigger the build process for a completed self-build.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click it to initiate a new build run based on the current state, overriding the 'never re-triggers' rule of the standard review card.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific note yet.

howto.what_you_can_do: Read the warning that the build will not automatically relaunch upon completion.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the build-review region (runtime/suite.py:review_verdicts); changing the trigger logic requires a code update there.
howto.what: The 'surface touched' trigger on the design review card.
howto.what_you_can_do: Touching this surface initiates the design review process for the item.

howto.what: The layers control on the build-review card.
howto.what_you_can_do: Open the detailed lifecycle states and failure reasons for the current build review.
